title: refactor(gen-text): remove dead code in handler, transforms, prompts, and auth
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- handler: drop unreachable async-iterable streaming path; keep only the
  ReadableStream return and the defensive null-body SSE fallback (same payload)
- handler/genericOpenAIClient: remove unused userInfo gateway context bag and
  its strip entry; keep referrer/isPrivate strips (still leak-guarded)
- createGeminiToolsTransform: make toolNames required, drop unused url_context
  default (url_context stays valid in shared/sdk/mcp; just not a per-model default)
- prompts/auth/portkey/imageUrl: remove dead `character` base prompt, unused
  service-account project_id, redundant additionalHeaders default, and a
  single-use byte-limit alias

Risks: none — pure dead-code removal, no callers, no behavior change. The null
streaming fallback emits the identical SSE payload; tsc and the
genericOpenAIClient + error-observability suites pass.

_~69 net lines removed · biome ✓ · cd gen.pollinations.ai && npx vitest run test/text/genericOpenAIClient.test.ts test/error- ✓_

gist.headline: Spring cleaning the text API
gist.blurb: We did some spring cleaning in the text generation API, sweeping away unused code and unreachable paths. A lighter codebase means a happier hive!
gist.summary: Removed approximately 69 lines of dead code across the text generation handler, transforms, prompts, and auth modules, including an unreachable streaming path and unused context variables.
gist.impact: The text generation codebase is now leaner and easier to maintain, with zero changes to existing API behavior or SSE payloads.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee wearing a tiny janitor's hat, sweeping up pixelated code snippets. The Nomnom creature happily eating the swept-up dead code.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny janitor's hat, sweeping up glowing pixelated code snippets into a dustpan. The round tan Nomnom creature sits nearby, happily eating the swept-up dead code. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
